title: 10 best herbal creams for constipation

remedy_properties: Senna glycosides: Senna glycosides, specifically sennoside A and B, have a laxative effect by stimulating bowel movements and increasing gut motility, helping to relieve constipation.

Tannins: Tannins in cassia cream help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to chronic constipation, and also aid in improving stool texture and frequency.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in cassia cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing oxidative stress and promoting a healthy gut microbiome, both of which can contribute to improved bowel function.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Senna alexandrina, also known as cassia, creams helps with constipation because they contain anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and increase muscle contractions in the intestines. These active compounds help to break down stool and reduce the time it takes for food to pass through the digestive system. As a result, Senna alexandrina creams can help to alleviate constipation by promoting regular bowel movements and restoring a healthy balance to the digestive system. This can provide relief from uncomfortable symptoms and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinone glycosides: These compounds stimulate bowel movements, increase gut motility, and promote the elimination of waste, thereby relieving constipation.

Sennosides: Sennosides are anthraquinone glycosides that are directly responsible for the laxative properties of Senna alexandrina, as they are converted into anthraquinone aglycones in the gut, inducing a cathartic effect.

Emodin: Emodin is an anthraquinone aglycone that has a direct effect on the gut, stimulating the secretion of water and electrolytes, which helps to soften and move stool through the digestive system, alleviating constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anthraquinones, which are used to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Leaves: The leaves contain sennosides, which are anthraquinone glycosides that help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Seeds: The seeds of Senna alexandrina are also used to treat constipation due to their anthraquinone content, which helps to stimulate bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Dry the senna alexandrina pods in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried pods into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the ground pods with 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 180°f for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the cream into a clean container and let it cool to room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Laxative action: Dandelion root extract has been shown to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation due to its ability to increase muscle contractions in the intestine and improve gut motility.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and chronic constipation.

Digestive enzyme stimulant: The flavonoids present in dandelion flowers have been found to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, including bile and pancreatic juices, which aid in breaking down nutrients and promoting regular bowel movements.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with constipation because it contains inulin and other compounds that stimulate the digestive system. The roots of the dandelion plant have been used for centuries to aid in bowel movements and relieve constipation. The cream's emollient and soothing properties also help to soften stool and make it easier to pass, providing relief from constipation and promoting a healthy digestive system. Regular use can also help to establish a regular bowel movement schedule.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that acts as a prebiotic, feeding beneficial gut bacteria and promoting a healthy gut microbiome, which can help to regulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation.

Flavonoids (such as quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting the movement of food through the digestive system.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpenoid saponin that has been shown to have laxative effects, increasing gut motility and water absorption in the intestines, which can help to relieve constipation and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are rich in inulin, a natural prebiotic that helps promote gut health and relieve constipation.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are high in potassium, which can help regulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds are a good source of oil, which can be used to create topical creams that help stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 250g of fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the early morning to obtain maximum potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve medicinal properties.
Combine 100g of dried taraxacum officinale roots with 200g of beeswax in a double boiler to create a base mixture.
Add 50g of coconut oil and 20g of shea butter to the base mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the final mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Digestive enzymes: Ginger contains digestive enzymes like zingibain, which helps to break down proteins and fats, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting regular bowel movements.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the gut lining, allowing for smoother muscle contractions and easier passage of stool.

Spasmolytic properties: The volatile oils present in ginger, such as citral and geraniol, have spasmolytic effects, which can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate constipation symptoms like bloating and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with constipation because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stimulate digestion. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to increase gut motility and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This can help to prevent constipation by promoting regular bowel movements and relieving symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These volatile compounds, particularly [6]-gingerol, help relax the smooth muscles in the intestines, reducing spasms and improving bowel movement.
Shogaols: These bioactive compounds, similar to gingerols, also help relax the intestinal muscles, reducing inflammation and improving the motility of the gut, thus alleviating constipation.

Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote the movement of food through the digestive system, thereby relieving constipation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have natural laxative properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make creams for constipation, as they contain compounds that stimulate digestion and relieve bowel movements.
Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used to make creams, as they have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in late summer or early fall when the plants start to yellow.
Steam the rhizomes for 10-15 minutes to extract the active compounds and soften the outer skin.
Grind 200g of the steamed rhizomes into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Combine the ground zingiber officinale paste with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined and smooth.

remedy_properties: Fiber content: Psyllium cream contains a significant amount of fiber, which helps to add bulk to stool and promote regular bowel movements, reducing the likelihood of constipation.

Glycemic control: Herbal psyllium creams have been shown to slow down the absorption of sugar into the bloodstream, regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity, which can help alleviate constipation symptoms.

Prebiotic properties: The prebiotic fibers in psyllium cream feed beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome, which is essential for proper digestion and bowel function, reducing the risk of constipation.

remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their high mucilage content, which helps to soften and lubricate the stool, making it easier to pass.
Leaves: Used for their mucilaginous properties, which can help to soothe and calm the digestive system.
Roots: Used for their fiber content, which can help to promote regular bowel movements and prevent constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried plantago ovata leaves and flowers from a clean environment.
Grind the plantago ovata leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Steep 100g of the powder in 500ml of distilled water at 90c for 10 minutes then cool.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 10g of cocoa butter with 50g of the cooled herbal infusion in a double boiler.
Stir and heat the mixture at 60c for 30 minutes then remove and let cool to form a cream.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soften and lubricate stool, making it easier to pass through the digestive tract, by reducing inflammation in the gut and promoting healthy gut bacteria.

Saponins: Stimulates the movement of food through the digestive system, increasing bowel contractions and regularity, and also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce pain and discomfort associated with constipation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and clean them thoroughly with lukewarm distilled water.
Infuse the roots in 500 milliliters of lukewarm distilled water for 2 hours to release their active ingredients.
Strain the liquid and combine it with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir constantly for 10 minutes until the beeswax and coconut oil melt.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

Combine the ground curcuma longa powder with 20g of shea butter in a mixing bowl.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of ulmus rubra bark, dried and powdered, and store in an airtight container for later use.

Combine the infused oil with 50g of beeswax, 20g of candelilla wax, and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the waxes melt and the mixture reaches 40°c in 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with a pair of clean scissors for best results.

Strain the infused oil through a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids immediately.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of a base cream containing ingredients like beeswax and shea butter thoroughly.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10% ginkgo biloba extract 120ml, 20% beeswax 40g, 10% shea butter 20g, and 60% coconut oil 120ml.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at 180-190°f for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Combine the ginkgo biloba extract and shea butter in a bowl and mix until well combined.
Gradually add the melted beeswax to the ginkgo biloba mixture and mix until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow it to set.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with constipation because they contain natural ingredients that stimulate digestive processes, soften stool, and reduce inflammation in the colon. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to the desired yield and quality standards.
Combine 20 grams of chopped sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a dark glass container.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking the container daily, until the herbs infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Mix the filtered oil with 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 milliliters of coconut oil, melting the mixture in a double boiler under low heat for 30-45 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers from the wild or your garden.
Dry the harvested herbs in a cool dark place for at least 3 days to reduce moisture content.
Blend 1 cup of dried herbs with 1/4 cup of sweet almond oil in a slow cooker for 6 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine 10ml of the herbal oil with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax melts and emulsifies, then pour into glass jars and cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with constipation because of its soothing properties that ease digestion and relaxation of the muscles in the intestinal tract. remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and increasing the frequency of bowel movements. This allows for easier passage of stool and alleviates symptoms of constipation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning when the oil content is highest for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels and letting them air dry for 24 hours.
Combine 100g of dried peppermint leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil and infuse the mixture in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.

Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids then add 20% beeswax and melt the mixture over low heat until it reaches a temperature of 120°f.
Pour the melted cream base into sterilized jars and label them with the date and contents then store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months before use.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to soften stool and stimulate bowel movements.
Seeds: Contain a high amount of mucilage, a natural laxative that helps to soothe and lubricate the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trifolium pratense leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated containing 20g of dried material.
Combine dried trifolium pratense leaves with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing for maceration and infusion to occur slowly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids completely.
Mix 50ml of the herbal oil extract with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil heating gently until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock creams helps with constipation because it contains saponins, a type of phytochemical that stimulates bowel movements and improves digestion. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with constipation. Additionally, curly dock's natural fibers help to bulk up stool, making it easier to pass and preventing straining during bowel movements. This natural remedy can provide relief from chronic constipation without relying on harsh medications.
remedy_properties: Anthraquinones: These compounds stimulate bowel movements, increase stool frequency, and soften stool texture to alleviate constipation symptoms.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative acts as a natural laxative, increasing intestinal motility and reducing spasms in the digestive tract to promote regular bowel movements.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the gut lining and reduce inflammation associated with constipation, making it easier for stool to pass through the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of curly dock contain anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and help relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: Curly dock rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of constipation.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried rumex crispus leaves and stems and combine them with enough water to create a strong infusion.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix equal parts of the infused liquid with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency and cools slightly.
Transfer the cream to airtight containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with constipation because of its natural ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and relieve spasms in the colon. The cream's active compound, thujone, helps to increase gut motility, allowing for regular and easy bowel movements. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with constipation. As a result, herbal wormwood creams can provide effective relief from constipation symptoms, promoting a healthy and regular bowel routine.
remedy_properties: Irritant effects: Wormwood's bitter compounds stimulate the digestive tract, increasing contractions and movement to help relieve constipation by promoting regular bowel movements.
Antispasmodic properties: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenes help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms and cramping that can contribute to constipation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Wormwood's polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote a healthy gut environment that supports regular bowel function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wormwood roots have a stimulating effect on the digestive system, making them effective in relieving constipation symptoms.
Barks: The barks of wormwood contain bioactive compounds like thujone and camphor, which help to increase gut motility and alleviate constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 70°f for 24 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered herb with 8 tablespoons of cold-pressed coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool to around 140°f, then whip the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk for about 5 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream, ready for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng creams helps with constipation because of its unique blend of herbs that stimulate digestive function, improve gut motility, and relax the muscles in the colon. The cream's ingredients work together to increase peristalsis, reducing symptoms such as bloating and discomfort associated with irregular bowel movements. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the digestive tract, making it easier for the body to eliminate waste regularly.
remedy_properties: Saponin content: Siberian Ginseng contains saponins, which have been shown to stimulate bowel movements and improve stool quality by increasing the frequency and consistency of bowel movements.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant compounds present in Siberian Ginseng cream help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of constipation and promoting regular bowel function.

Adaptogenic activity: Siberian Ginseng is known for its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in physiological processes, including digestion and bowel function, thereby alleviating symptoms of constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, the roots are used to stimulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including polysaccharides and polyphenols, which help to regulate gut motility and alleviate constipation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root powder into a clean glass bowl.
Combine the powder with 40 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes to melt the beeswax and mix thoroughly with an immersion blender.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before adding 20 drops of lavender essential oil and 15 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Stir well and pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars filling them about 3/4 full.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for constipation are topical ointments containing herbal extracts that help soften and stimulate bowel movements, relieving constipation symptoms. The best herbal creams for constipation include Cassia angustifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Rheum palmatum, Foeniculum vulgare, Zingiber officinale, Terminalia chebula, Magnolia officinalis, Rheum officinale, and Senna alexandrina. If left untreated, constipation can lead to bloating, fatigue, and abdominal pain, affecting daily activities, work productivity, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to constipation that are treated with herbal creams are hemorrhoids, anal fissures, and piles. Herbal creams containing aloe vera, witch hazel, and chamomile can soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other herbal remedies like calendula and arnica may help promote healing and reduce pain associated with these conditions. These natural ingredients offer a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing symptoms and promoting overall digestive health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with constipation is a blend of aloe vera, triphala, and chamomile. Aloe vera soothes and calms the digestive tract, while triphala stimulates bowel movement and promotes regularity. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to ease any discomfort or pain associated with constipation. Together, these creams work synergistically to promote a healthy digestive system, reduce inflammation, and encourage regular bowel movements. This combination can be applied topically to the abdomen or taken orally as directed.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The American Journal of Chinese Medicine, the researchers reviewed and summarized the major chemical components from rhubarb, senna leaf, and aloe, which are frequently used herbal medications for achieving regular bowel movement. The study found that anthraquinone, especially its dianthrone compounds such as sennoside A and sennoside B, possess significant effects to promote gastrointestinal motility and relieve functional constipation. Herbal creams containing these compounds may be beneficial for constipation as they can help stimulate bowel movements and relieve symptoms associated with constipation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia creams helps with constipation because they contain sennosides, a group of anthraquinone glycosides derived from the plant's roots. These compounds have a laxative effect by stimulating the bowel muscles and increasing water and electrolyte secretion in the intestines, leading to a bowel movement. The creams also help to soften stool and improve bowel motility, making it easier to pass stool and relieving constipation symptoms. Regular use of Cassia angustifolia creams can provide relief from constipation and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_constituents: Sennosides: These anthraquinone glycosides stimulate bowel movements by increasing the motility of the intestines and promoting the movement of stool through the digestive system.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have a laxative effect by increasing the water content in the stool, making it softer and easier to pass, and also by increasing the movement of the intestines.
Flavonoids: These compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing stool to pass more easily and relieving constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high sennoside content, which helps stimulate bowel movements.
Leaves: Utilized for their anthraquinone content, which has a laxative effect to treat constipation.
Seeds: Employed for their sennoside and anthraquinone content, contributing to the laxative properties of the creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10g of dried cassia angustifolia leaves in 100ml of hot water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids add 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 40°c then add 10ml of sweet almond oil.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with constipation because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The cream's natural emollient properties help to soften and lubricate the bowel, making it easier to pass stool. Additionally, Aloe vera's prebiotic properties promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help to regulate bowel movements and improve overall digestive health, providing relief from constipation symptoms. This natural remedy can provide quick and effective relief from constipation.
remedy_constituents: Glycoproteins: These help with constipation by increasing intestinal motility and water absorption in the colon, promoting regular bowel movements.
Polysaccharides: These help with constipation by increasing the water content in the stool and enhancing intestinal motility, making it easier to pass stool.
Anthraquinones: These help with constipation by stimulating the smooth muscle in the intestines to contract and move the stool along, relieving constipation symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of aloin, a natural laxative compound that helps to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high content of glycoproteins, which helps to soften stool and stimulate bowel movements, making it easier to pass stool.
Leaves: They are also used due to their high content of mucilages, which helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel in a stainless steel container.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add 10 grams of shea butter to the saucepan and continue heating for another 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before whipping.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with constipation because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream may help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the intestines, allowing for easier passage of stool. Additionally, the licorice root extract has been shown to increase the secretion of digestive enzymes and bile, which can help stimulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation. This natural remedy may provide relief from constipation and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It acts as a laxative by inhibiting the activity of aldosterone, which in turn increases the secretion of water and electrolytes in the intestines, thereby softening stool and promoting bowel movements.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoflavones): They help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and improve gut motility, allowing for more efficient movement of food through the digestive system and relieving constipation.
Saponins: Saponins have a gentle laxative effect by increasing the secretion of mucus in the intestines, which helps soften stool and make it easier to pass, thereby relieving constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for constipation because they contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and laxative properties.
Leaves: Leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for constipation because they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, but also other beneficial compounds that aid in digestion.

Seeds: Seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for constipation because they contain additional compounds that can help stimulate bowel movements and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 100ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Combine the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes and stir occasionally until the beeswax and shea butter melt.
Pour the mixture into a sterile container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum palmatum creams helps with constipation because they contain rhubarb extract, which is a natural laxative. The sennosides present in rhubarb stimulate the intestines to move and increase the frequency of bowel movements. Additionally, Rheum palmatum creams may also help to soften stool and improve digestion, making it easier to pass waste. Its natural properties can also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, promoting a healthy gut and relieving symptoms of constipation. Regular use can lead to improved bowel regularity.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds stimulate bowel movements by increasing the frequency and strength of contractions in the intestinal tract, thereby alleviating constipation.
Tannins: Tannins have a mild laxative effect and help to soften stool, making it easier to pass, and also increase the water content in the intestines to help prevent constipation.

Alkaloids (Rhein and Palmatin): These alkaloids have a direct impact on the intestinal tract, stimulating peristalsis and increasing the secretion of intestinal mucus, which helps to facilitate the smooth passage of stool and alleviate constipation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain anthraquinone glycosides, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Leaves: They also contain anthraquinone glycosides, similar to roots, which help to stimulate bowel movements and treat constipation.
Rhyzomes: They contain sennosides, which are anthraquinone glycosides that stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried rheum palmatum root and 20g of beeswax to make the herbal cream.
Infuse 200ml of coconut oil with 30g of dried rheum palmatum root for 2 hours in a double boiler.
Strain the infused coconut oil and discard the solids then combine with 20g of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 20 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with constipation because they contain the active compounds anethole and fenchone, which have a natural relaxant effect on the muscles in the digestive tract. These compounds also help to regulate bowel movements and stimulate the muscles to contract, promoting regular and smooth passage of stool. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Foeniculum vulgare creams may help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing discomfort and promoting overall digestive health. This can lead to a more regular and healthy bowel routine.
remedy_constituents: Furanocoumarins: These compounds help stimulate bowel movement and relieve constipation by increasing the muscle contractions in the intestines and improving gut motility.

Terpenes (specifically Fenchone and Anethole): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help relax the intestinal muscles and reduce inflammation, making it easier to pass stool and alleviate constipation.

Phenolic acids (specifically Ferulic acid): These compounds have laxative properties and help increase the secretion of digestive enzymes, which aid in the breakdown of food and promote the movement of stool through the intestines, relieving constipation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are rich in oils, particularly anethole, which has been shown to stimulate digestive activity and ease constipation symptoms.
Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves contain a high concentration of volatile oils, including anethole, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and promote regular bowel movements.
Stems: Foeniculum vulgare stems contain a significant amount of coumarin, a natural antispasmodic that can help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate constipation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency and aroma.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine dried foeniculum vulgare with 30 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted and combined.
Pour the cooled mixture into a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with constipation because of its natural anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties. The active compound, gingerol, present in the cream stimulates digestion, improves gut motility, and relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract. This helps to regulate bowel movements and alleviate symptoms of constipation. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with constipation, promoting a healthy and regular bowel function.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help with constipation by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting smoother and easier bowel movements.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols help with constipation by reducing inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to constipation.

Volatile oils (specifically Zingiberene): These oils help with constipation by stimulating digestive enzymes and improving gut motility, which helps move food through the digestive system more efficiently.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Zingiber officinale contain gingerols, which have been shown to stimulate digestion and relieve constipation.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are a rich source of gingerols and shogaols, which help to relax muscles and stimulate bowel movements.
Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale contain compounds that may help to alleviate digestive discomfort and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250g of fresh zingiber officinale roots in 500ml of carrier oil at 40-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Combine 100g of shea butter and 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 40-50 degrees celsius.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 20g of vitamin e oil with the shea butter and coconut oil mixture.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Terminalia chebula creams helps with constipation because of its natural laxative properties, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve digestive discomfort. The herb contains chebulic acid, a potent compound that enhances intestinal motility and relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract. This helps to ease the passage of stool, reduce straining during bowel movements, and promote regular bowel function. As a result, Terminalia chebula creams provide a gentle yet effective solution for individuals experiencing constipation and supporting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help with constipation by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the movement of food through the intestines, easing bowel movements.
Flavonoids: They help with constipation by enhancing the absorption of water and electrolytes in the intestines, softening stool, and increasing the frequency of bowel movements.

Polysaccharides: They help with constipation by increasing the water-holding capacity of stool, making it easier to pass, and stimulating the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can help regulate bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Used to make creams for constipation as they contain chebulic acid, which helps to soften stool and promote bowel movement.
Barks: Used to make creams for constipation as they contain chebulagic acid, which helps to stimulate bowel movement and relieve constipation.
Seeds: Used to make creams for constipation as they contain chebulic acid, which helps to soften stool and promote bowel movement.
remedy_recipe: Weigh terminalia chebula powder 100 grams and mix it with 100 grams of cocoa butter in a stainless steel bowl.
Melt the cocoa butter and terminalia chebula mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Remove the bowl from the heat source and add 50 grams of beeswax to the mixture.
Stir the mixture well until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the temperature cools down to 140 to 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the mixture into a glass container and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Magnolia officinalis creams helps with constipation because of its soothing and gentle laxative properties. The natural compounds present in Magnolia officinalis, such as magnolol and honokiol, have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to promote digestive health and relieve constipation. These compounds help to stimulate the muscles in the digestive tract, increasing peristalsis and promoting regular bowel movements. As a result, Magnolia officinalis creams can provide relief from constipation and promote a healthy digestive system. Regular use can also help to prevent future episodes of constipation.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation by increasing the contractions of the intestinal muscles.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Magnolia officinalis have laxative effects, helping to soften stool and promote its passage through the intestines.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids, particularly those with anti-inflammatory properties, can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and promote regular bowel movements.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used because of their rich content of iridoids, which have been found to have a laxative effect.
Barks: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and relax the digestive system.
Leaves: They are used because of their mild laxative properties and their ability to stimulate the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried magnolia officinalis bark from a reputable supplier to ensure quality.
Steep the dried magnolia officinalis bark in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the infused liquid and the melted beeswax mixture then allow to cool and thicken for several hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum officinale creams helps with constipation because of its unique properties that stimulate bowel movements and relax the muscles in the digestive tract. The cream's active compounds, such as rhein and emodin, work to increase the frequency and ease of bowel movements, reducing the duration of constipation episodes. Additionally, Rheum officinale creams have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and facilitating the regular passage of stool. This natural approach can provide relief from constipation.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have laxative properties, helping to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation by increasing water and electrolyte absorption in the intestines.
Triterpenoids: These compounds help to soften stool, making it easier to pass, and also increase gut motility, which helps to move stool through the digestive system.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, promoting regular bowel movements and relieving constipation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make creams for constipation due to their high content of anthraquinones, which help stimulate bowel movements.
Roots: Roots are used to make creams for constipation due to their rich source of anthraquinones and saponins, which help soften stool and promote regular bowel movements.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make creams for constipation due to their content of anthraquinones, flavonoids, and other compounds that help stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rheum officinale roots in early summer morning when dew is still present on leaves.
Clean and dry the harvested roots by gently brushing off dirt with soft brush for 10 minutes.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and mix with 200g of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-70°c for 2 hours to facilitate the infusion process.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then whip with 100g of beeswax at 40-50°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Senna alexandrina creams helps with constipation because they contain anthraquinone glycosides, which are natural laxatives that stimulate bowel movements. These compounds help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, increasing the movement of food and waste through the intestines. As a result, Senna alexandrina creams can help to relieve constipation by promoting regular bowel movements and reducing the symptoms associated with constipation, such as bloating and discomfort. This can lead to improved overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These constituents stimulate bowel movements by increasing the intensity and frequency of contractions, helping to relieve constipation.

Alkaloids: Specifically, the alkaloids present in Senna alexandrina, such as sennoside A and sennoside B, increase the water content in the intestines and stimulate peristalsis, making it easier to pass stool.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin and Kaempferol): These constituents help to increase the secretion of bile and intestinal mucus, which aids in the movement of stool through the digestive system and also helps to soften the stool.
remedy_parts: Roots: Senna alexandrina roots are used due to their high content of anthraquinone glycosides, which have a laxative effect.
Leaves: Senna alexandrina leaves are used for their emodin content, which helps to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation.
Rhyzomes: Senna alexandrina rhyzomes are used because they contain a mixture of anthraquinone glycosides that help to soften stool and promote bowel movements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 senna alexandrina flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency and minimal moisture content.
Dry the harvested senna alexandrina flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30-40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Steep 20 grams of dried senna alexandrina flowers and leaves in 1 liter of boiling water for 30 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the senna alexandrina infusion and combine it with 150 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes until it thickens and turns into a smooth, creamy consistency suitable for application.
